category_title: Chinese immigrant students with ADHD and English-language dominance
minimal_context: Eric is sitting at the computer screen for an individual Chinese language lesson to learn new vocabulary.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Look at the computer screen. We are going to learn new Chinese vocabulary today. Please listen to my instructions.

STUDENT: What did you just say?

TUTOR: I am explaining the vocabulary. You need to focus so that you can progress at the same pace as your peers.

STUDENT: Can I go play now?

TUTOR: No, we need to finish this lesson first. Please sit and listen.

STUDENT: (Gets out of seat and goes to the play area of the classroom)

options.A: Transition the decision of when and how long to visit the play area from the tutor to the student.
options.B: Adopt the same sequence of learning activities in each individual lesson with a sticker-system.
options.C: Provide opportunities to respond to the new vocabulary at a rate of four to six responses per minute.
options.D: Provide a quieter room for the individual lessons with comfortable chairs away from the computer screen.
